_comment: Citation eval cases (Phase 14, core — CC-authored on owner override, owner-reviewed at the merge gate). A case = {id, query, cited_atom}; pass = some memo line containing the atom carries a [chunk:N] tag AND a cited chunk contains the atom. Atom rules (learned from live failures): (1) DOCUMENT-ONLY — if any other tool can produce the fact (tickers -> price history!), the model will source it there, untagged, correctly: prefer people/venues/deals over tickers and numbers; (2) UNIQUELY TIED to one source context, or the model attaches a neighboring chunk's tag; (3) the query may NAME the subject (the gate tests attribution, not spontaneous surfacing) but naming a ticker steers the model to price tools — see rule 1. Inspect the corpus with: uv run python -m eval.corpus <term>. Re-author when the corpus refreshes (known maintenance).

cases.query: what upcoming AMD events could move the stock?
what does recent coverage report about Warren Buffett's plans for his Berkshire stake?
how does AMD's revenue picture compare with Navitas Semiconductor?
which semiconductor equipment suppliers raised their forecasts recently and why does it matter for the AI trade?
what recently reported compute deals involve Nvidia chips?
